channel.title: Cricket on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Cricket on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Sunday's Guyana Brief - Hotels Multiply, Health Goes Digital, and Cricket Women Keep Winning
item.description: Another US$18M hotel opens, your medical records go online, President Ali says cash grants aren’t sustainable (after promising one), Women’s cricket squad stays undefeated, and Fruta Conquerors lose their president after 11 months.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Responds: Hotels Mean JOBS, Women Cricketers Making Us PROUD, and Stop Complaining!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Queens celebrates the Women’s T20 squad, defends the hotel boom, explains why digital health records are PROGRESS, and asks why the Brief can’t just be happy for once.

item.title: Saturday's Guyana Brief: Toy Guns Guarding Your Groceries, Solar Power Rising, and Guyana Women Battle Jamaica Tonight!
item.description: Security guards caught with toy guns at supermarkets, US$8.14M solar farm lights up Charity, GBTI launches private banking for the rich, and Guyana Women seek redemption against Jamaica under the lights!

item.title: Cricket Dreams: How Guyana's Women Are Rewriting Sports History
item.description: While politics divides, Guyana’s women’s cricket team unites. This is the story of how a group of determined athletes became the nation’s most powerful symbol of what we can achieve together.
